title: Rei and Kaworus Heart Shadows
wikitext:
[]: Rei and Kaworu's Heart Shadows             Quote Post by Ari » Wed Apr 15, 2015 12:03 am I am probably not the first one to notice, but Rei and Kaworu's shadows are pretty clearly heart-shaped in the post-instrumentality scene in the End of Evangelion. What significance does it have? [/img]

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Wed Apr 15, 2015 12:08 am They were the only ones who ever loved Shinji unconditionally?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Apr 15, 2015 12:21 am If it means anything (not just a weird animation quirk), I'd honestly agree with that. Though I think Asuka and Misato cared for Shinji too, both had their own motivates for doing so. It wasn't unconditional.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 15, 2015 4:18 am What about Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Apr 15, 2015 2:12 pm Both of them are basically divine personifications of unconditional love for all living things, Yui can suck it.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 15, 2015 4:00 pm OOC, what shape is Shinji's shadow? It's hard to see with that subtitle.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Apr 15, 2015 4:04 pm I entered this topic hoping, against my expectation, to find something cute and complimentary to the gods, and I was surprised to find my hope realized. Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Thu Apr 16, 2015 4:07 pm Sachi wrote: What about Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Apr 16, 2015 4:09 pm Sachi wrote: What about Yui?

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Thu Apr 16, 2015 4:44 pm Ray wrote: Yui is a giant purple Lovecraftian demon who abandoned her own son! She doesn't count!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 16, 2015 4:48 pm Rewatch the flashback scene in Episode 21 (Director's Cut) coupled with the end of that flashback featured in EoE. Flows together nicely and shows Yui's intent* was two-fold: Third Impact on her terms (not Seele's) since it's inevitable, and Eva becoming a testament to humanity. Life, not death like Seele's wishes. *Whether or not she was fully sane being stuck in the Eva/becoming Eva-01 for after so many years is another argument for another thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Shoujo Kakumei Asuka » Thu Apr 16, 2015 4:48 pm This part still seems so disconnected with the last scene of the movie. I mean, I know this was deliberate, but still... I'd agree on this meaning for Rei and Kaworu's shadow here. The heart shaped shadows are both emblematic of unconditional love shown to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Thu Apr 16, 2015 5:15 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Rewatch the flashback scene in Episode 21 (Director's Cut) coupled with the end of that flashback featured in EoE. Flows together nicely and shows Yui's intent* was two-fold: Third Impact on her terms (not Seele's) since it's inevitable, and Eva becoming a testament to humanity. Life, not death like Seele's wishes. *Whether or not she was fully sane being stuck in the Eva/becoming Eva-01 for after so many years is another argument for another thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Apr 16, 2015 6:17 pm Lavinius wrote: I entered this topic hoping, against my expectation, to find something cute and complimentary to the gods, and I was surprised to find my hope realized. Thank you.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 16, 2015 10:00 pm Dream wrote: Honestly, i find something painfully ironic yet sublimely beautiful about the fact the only people to have shown Shinji absolute, pure love weren't even human.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 16, 2015 10:36 pm Dream wrote: Honestly, i find something painfully ironic yet sublimely beautiful about the fact the only people to have shown Shinji absolute, pure love weren't even human.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 16, 2015 10:39 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Reichu's technicalities aside

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 16, 2015 10:43 pm Oh! I just meant in the sense that they're human, but their original forms were aliens (part of the FAR), etc.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Apr 17, 2015 12:12 am They're human, but they're fundamentally disconnected from the Human Experience that the homo sapien is familiar with and which a work of fiction can discuss and analyze. They're 'alien' in a philosophical sense regardless of how technically human they are.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 17, 2015 12:23 am AuraTwilight wrote: They're human, but they're fundamentally disconnected from the Human Experience that the homo sapien is familiar with and which a work of fiction can discuss and analyze. They're 'alien' in a philosophical sense regardless of how technically human they are.
